summary: DHH celebrates Trump's return to the presidency, noting that two-thirds of young Americans (18-29) are optimistic about the next four years. He frames the moment as bigger than Trump himself—a cultural shift away from excessive self-criticism toward renewed American confidence. He contrasts American optimism with European pessimism, expressing hope that Europe will eventually follow suit. He acknowledges the piece is more faith than analysis, but declares optimism the only viable stance.
key_points: Young Americans (18-29) are the most optimistic demographic about Trump's second term, with two-thirds expressing positive outlook
The cultural shift transcends politics—it's economic, entrepreneurial, and technological in nature
Europe is stuck in deep pessimism and regulatory thinking, falling further behind America's dynamism
Europe was economically competitive with America as recently as 15 years ago but lacks America's ability to course-correct
DHH openly admits this is faith-based optimism rather than rigorous analysis
money_quotes: After spending so long lost in the wilderness of excessive self-criticism and self-loathing, there's finally a broad coalition of the willing working to get the mojo back.
This is what's so exhilarating about America. The big, dramatic swings. The high stakes. The long shots.
I like this country much better when it's confident in that inherent national character.
As someone with a foot on both the American and European continent, I can't help being jealous with my euro leg.

I can also well imagine Europe sticking with Eurocrat thinking, spinning its wheels with grand but empty proclamations, issuing scorning but impotent admonishments of America, and doubling down on the regulatory black hole.
Optimism must win. Pessimism is literally for losers.
This is far more an article of faith than of analysis, mind you.
key_insight: American optimism under Trump represents a broader cultural shift beyond politics—toward entrepreneurial confidence and away from self-loathing—that Europe desperately needs but may lack the agility to replicate.
